_doc: ADR-006 ratchet baseline. Counting rule: non-comment, line-leading 'record_result ' call sites in validate.sh and 'Add-Result ' in validate.ps1 (regex ^\s*record_result / ^\s*Add-Result). Helper-internal calls are a constant offset absorbed here. New checks MUST go through run_python_check / Invoke-PythonCheck (which add zero such lines). Raising a count above original_floor requires a one-line entry in 'justifications' naming the check and why it must run without Python (ADR-006 escape hatch). Lowering counts (migration) requires updating these numbers in the same change. KNOWN SCOPE LIMIT: mid-line emission styles (cmd && record_result / || { record_result } / if(){ Add-Result }) are deliberately uncounted — that authoring style for NEW checks evades the machine ratchet and is caught at review (spec Enforcement Boundary; 4 such pre-existing sites live today).
justifications: token lifecycle drift advisory (issue #157 / backlog #51): 5 native record_result + 5 native Add-Result sites emit WARN/PASS/SKIP for governance token-cost drift. Must be WARN (advisory, never FAIL); run_python_check / Invoke-PythonCheck map exit!=0 -> FAIL and cannot express WARN. Mirrors the governance-eval-coverage advisory precedent. Hard teeth live in tests/ci/test_lifecycle_baseline_drift.py.
